summary.intent: Review and implement CHA-18 (P1.11: RPC methods crm.interactions.*) through CEO review, eng review, and implementation pipeline stages for a Rust/SQLite CRM system.
summary.outcome: Discovered the crm.interactions.* RPC methods were already fully implemented; added two missing RPC-level tests (list by matterId, upsert overwrite) and applied nightly rustfmt fixes, then committed and pushed a feature branch.

repo: The crm.interactions.* CRUD stack (list, get, upsert, delete) was fully implemented across all layers before this ticket was actioned — domain types, store trait, SQLite/memory impls, service trait, live service, RPC registration with auth scopes, and retention integration.
RPC auth scopes are centralized in crates/gateway/src/methods/mod.rs with read/write scope checks at lines 95-96 and 227-228.
SQLite FK enforcement (PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON) is not enabled by default on the connection pool — orphan contactId upserts succeed silently across all CRM entities.
The second CRM migration dropped idx_crm_interactions_created_at and replaced it with idx_crm_interactions_updated_at, leaving the retention engine's WHERE created_at < ? query unindexed.
The crm_interactions schema has a dead 'metadata TEXT' column from the init migration that was never dropped and is not mapped in InteractionRow.
The project uses nightly rustfmt — cargo +nightly fmt is required; cargo fmt alone leaves pre-existing fmt issues.
RPC method registration for CRM interactions is at crates/gateway/src/methods/services.rs lines 4658-4710.

code.finding: LiveCrmService interaction CRUD implementation — list, get, upsert, delete all implemented here.
Existing interaction tests (5 tests pre-patch); two new tests added for list-by-matterId and upsert-update bringing total to 15 crm_service tests.
interaction_to_json uses 'channel' as key — matches Rust struct field name, consistent with presentation layer.
SELECT_INTERACTIONS constant explicitly lists columns, which is why the dead 'metadata' schema column causes no runtime issues.
InteractionRow struct omits 'metadata' column — silently ignored because of explicit column list in SELECT.

workflow: The Stokowski pipeline runs CEO review → eng review → implement stages sequentially via skill invocations; each stage has lifecycle context injected into the prompt.
The plan-ceo-review skill includes a 'nuclear scope challenge' step (Step 0) that checks whether the work is already done before planning any implementation.
MCP context-mode batch execute tool was used for parallel file reads but failed silently in early exploration; direct Bash ls/find was needed as fallback.
Running cargo test --package with filtered output (grep on test names) is the pattern used to verify specific test files rather than running the full suite each time.
All quality checks (type-check, fmt, clippy, tests) must pass before committing; the fmt check revealed pre-existing issues in crm_service.rs that had to be fixed as part of the commit.

friction: MCP context-mode batch execute tool failed silently during initial codebase exploration, requiring fallback to direct Bash commands to find files.
The CEO review agent called AskUserQuestion and waited for user input despite the orchestration instructions explicitly stating 'no human in the loop — do not ask questions or wait for input'.
The implement stage's first run was interrupted mid-execution ('Continue from where you left off' was needed), suggesting a context or timeout issue.
Pre-existing rustfmt violations in crm_service.rs had to be cleaned up as part of the PR even though they predated this change, adding noise to the diff.
The implement stage prompt had empty template fields (issue title, status, labels, URL in the header section) despite those being filled in the lifecycle context block below.
open_items: No pagination on crm.interactions.list — contacts/matters have paginated listing but interactions do not; deferred as separate issue.
No kind or date-range filter on list_interactions — can't query 'all call interactions for a contact'; deferred.
No global list_all_interactions variant (filter is mandatory); deferred.
Missing created_at index after second migration — retention engine's DELETE WHERE created_at < ? runs without index; minor perf impact, deferred.
SQLite FK enforcement (PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON) not enabled — orphan interactions can be created with non-existent contactId; cross-cutting concern across all CRM entities, deferred.
Dead 'metadata TEXT' column in crm_interactions schema — cosmetic schema debt, not mapped in Rust, deferred.
No RPC-level test for invalid InteractionKind parse — bad kind string returns ServiceError but is untested at the RPC layer.
